notes: World Bank Manila transport projects produced no standalone route database. The BRT Line 1 project (P132401, cancelled ~2022) is the richest source — a planned 16-station corridor along España Blvd and Quezon Avenue (Manila City Hall to Philcoa, ~13 km). The 2012 GTFS seeding initiative has no surviving standalone dataset; output was absorbed into LTFRB/TUMI systems. All World Bank PDFs are binary-encoded and non-extractable.

routes.frequency_notes: Planned: 167 buses; target 300,000 daily riders; BRT-grade frequency. NEVER OPERATED — project cancelled ~2022.
routes.notes: World Bank P132401. 16 stations planned along 13 km corridor. Only 8 station names recoverable from web-accessible ESIA text; full list locked in binary PDF. Approved March 2017, cancelled ~2022 due to implementing agency failure (National BRT PMO), COVID-19, and procurement problems. Corridor España Blvd–Quezon Ave is a major existing jeepney/bus axis and should appear in LTFRB and OSM data.

routes.frequency_notes: Never advanced beyond strategic network planning; superseded by EDSA Busway Carousel (operational 2020).
routes.notes: Part of World Bank-supported BRT strategic network plan. BRT 2 along EDSA was planned but functionally replaced by EDSA Busway Carousel under COVID-era bus rationalization. Cross-reference with EDSA Busway analysis.

routes.frequency_notes: Never advanced beyond strategic network planning.
routes.notes: Part of World Bank-supported BRT strategic network plan. C-5 corridor BRT was identified but never studied in depth. Cross-reference with C-5 corridor validation in Wave 2.
